commit.message:
Refactor and enhance Commissioning APIs (#3330)

* Enhance multi discovered device commissioning

We now try devices one after the other always one address and so give all devices a better chance to be tried within the timeout. We also ensure to continue (but remove the device) on PASE errors (e,g wrong passcode).

Also some code was streamlined between the legacy and node driven path. Also node driven commissionings can now handle multiple targets by chosing the relevant node depending on the commissioning result.

This change can also be a basis to easiely implement Multi-QR code commissioning in a later step

* formatting

* Adjust logic according to initial feedback

* Enhance multi-discovered device commissioning with parallel PASE and abort

- Rewrite CommissioningConnection as factory function with static and dynamic modes:
  - Static mode (pre-known addresses): launch all candidates immediately in parallel
  - Dynamic mode: poll discovered devices every 200ms, track permanently-failed addresses
  - Per-device AbortController map: UnexpectedDataError cancels sibling address attempts
  - Race loser cleanup: call initiateForceClose on losing PASE sessions to avoid 60s lockout
  - Discovery poll errors logged with context instead of silently swallowed
  - lastNonRetryableError tracks credential failures for clean UnexpectedDataError propagation

- Thread AbortSignal through the PASE stack:
  - PaseMessenger: abort-aware send/receive using Abort.race
  - PaseClient: abort.attempt() around each async step, sends InvalidParam on abort
  - ControllerCommissioner: abort? and continueCommissioningAfterPase? options
  - sendError intentionally not abort-bounded to notify device before closing

- Simplify CommissioningConnectionPool to credential tracking + availableCandidates()

- Add CommissioningDiscovery parallel commissioning:
  - Runs CommissioningClient.commission() per candidate in parallel
  - #paseWon flag gates the continueCommissioningAfterPase callback
  - Winner triggers abort signal to cancel remaining candidates
  - try/finally cleanup ensures pending promises resolve on error

- Add WiFi/Thread/regulatory credentials to CommissioningClient.BaseCommissioningOptions:
  - wifiNetwork?, threadNetwork?, regulatoryLocation?, regulatoryCountryCode?
  - Wired through CommissioningClient.commission() into LocatedNodeCommissioningOptions

- Add Peers.forDescriptor(descriptor: RemoteDescriptor): Promise<ClientNode>:
  - Uses ClientNodeFactory.find() to find an existing matching node
  - If found, refreshes addresses and discovery data via setStateOf
  - If not found, creates a new ClientNode via factory.create()
  - Uses RemoteDescriptor (new-style interface) instead of legacy CommissionableDevice
  - Follows the existing forAddress() naming pattern

- Migrate CommissioningController.commissionNode() to use new path for all routes:
  - Pre-discovered/known-address path: peers.forDescriptor() + clientNode.commission()
    replacing direct ControllerCommissioner.commission() + peers.forAddress()
  - Discovery data (SII/SAI/SAT) automatically propagated via CommissioningClient.descriptor
  - commissionedAt set by CommissioningClient.commission() (removed redundant setStateOf)
  - OTA provider location handled automatically by CommissioningClient
  - Pure-discovery path unchanged: CommissioningDiscovery via peers.commission()
  - Removes ControllerCommissioner, Time imports from CommissioningController
  - Fixes identifierData type narrowing in pure-discovery path

- Restore Outdoor regulatory default accidentally changed to Indoor

- Add 10 CommissioningConnection tests covering all major code paths

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* formatting

* formatting

* Fix PASE abort: don't abort after Pake3 is sent

Once Pake3 is sent the device considers the PASE session established.
Aborting at that point and sending InvalidParam is incorrect — the device
already committed to the session.

Remove abort.attempt() and abort signal from sendPasePake3, waitForSuccess,
and createSecureSession. These steps now complete unconditionally. If this
candidate is a loser in a parallel commissioning race, the caller's
continueCommissioningAfterPase callback will close the established session
cleanly instead.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Simplify forDescriptor: use agent.commissioning.descriptor setter

Use node.act() with the descriptor setter instead of manually calling
toLongForm() and setStateOf() — the setter already handles the mapping.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* refactor: unify commissioning dispatch; add finalizeCommissioning option; remove dead code

- CommissioningClient.BaseCommissioningOptions: add finalizeCommissioning? so the
  PASE-only callback (used by PaseCommissioner) can pass through peers.commission()
  and peers.forDescriptor() without a separate legacy code branch
- MatterController.commission(): all paths now go through peers.forDescriptor() or
  peers.commission(); completeCommissioningCallback is wrapped into finalizeCommissioning
  and passed down — ControllerCommissioner.commissionWithDiscovery() no longer needed
- ControllerCommissioner: remove commissionWithDiscovery() (was dead code)
- CommissioningController.commissionNode(): simplified — OTA setup + caseAuthenticatedTags
  default, then delegates to controller.commission(); remove ClientNode/RemoteDescriptor imports
- MatterController: remove DiscoveryAndCommissioningOptions import (unused); add PeerAddress
- ClientNodeTest: remove unnecessary commissioning loop exclusion — deepCopy preserves
  bigint primitives so expect.BIGINT/NUMBER/STRING matchers work in the loop
- FailsafeCommissioningTest: reduce this.timeout() from 30 s to 10 s (tests run in <1 s)

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix: thread configurable timeout through LocatedNodeCommissioningOptions; document UnexpectedDataError fallback

- LocatedNodeCommissioningOptions: add optional timeout field (defaults to Seconds(30),
  previously hardcoded) so callers can control the PASE establishment budget across
  all parallel address candidates
- CommissioningClient.BaseCommissioningOptions: expose timeout? and thread it through
  to LocatedNodeCommissioningOptions
- discoverAndEstablishPase: add explanatory comment for the intentional
  UnexpectedDataError swallow on known-address failures — stale/wrong known addresses
  may point to a different device; falling back to discovery handles that case

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* refactor: add PaseDiscovery + establishPase(); remove discoverAndEstablishPase and CommissioningConnection dynamic mode

- Remove discoverAndEstablishPase from ControllerCommissioner
- Add EstablishPaseOptions interface + establishPase() public method for
  known-address parallel PASE (no discovery)
- Remove CommissioningConnection dynamic mode (discoveredDevices callback,
  polling loop, permanentlyExcluded set) — now static-only
- Remove discoverDeviceAddressesByIdentifier from ControllerDiscovery
- Add PaseDiscovery extends Discovery<NodeSession> for scanner-lifecycle-
  managed parallel discovery+PASE; mirrors CommissioningDiscovery pattern
- Add Peers.pase() method returning a new PaseDiscovery instance
- Update MatterController.connectPaseChannel to use establishPase() for
  pre-discovered and known-address paths, and peers.pase() for discovery
- Update CommissioningConnectionTest: remove four tests that tested the
  now-removed dynamic mode

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* refactor: replace CommissionableDeviceDiscovery with inline ContinuousDiscovery wrapper

- Remove CommissionableDeviceDiscovery class and its export from @matter/node
- Remove Peers.discoverCommissionable() method
- CommissioningController and PaseCommissioner now construct ContinuousDiscovery
  directly and subscribe to discovery.discovered to convert ClientNode to
  CommissionableDevice via RemoteDescriptor.fromLongForm(node.state.commissioning)
- Deduplication by deviceIdentifier (falling back to address JSON) in both files
- Remove discoverCommissionableDevices and cancelCommissionableDeviceDiscovery
  from ControllerDiscovery (now fully dead code)

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* finalize

* fix: strip Discovery-internal fields from scanner identifier; review cleanups

Bugs fixed:
- Discovery.ts was passing the full Discovery.Options object (which
  includes timeout, scannerFilter, id) as the CommissionableDeviceIdentifiers
  to findCommissionableDevicesContinuously(). MdnsClient's empty-identifier
  branch is guarded by Object.keys(identifier).length === 0, so the extra
  fields caused it to skip the "return all commissioning devices" path,
  silently returning no results. Fix: destructure internal fields out before
  passing identifier to the scanner.

Review cleanups:
- CommissioningConnection: bound the post-abort cleanup wait to 5 seconds
  to prevent hanging if a transport ignores the abort signal
- ControllerCommissioner: extract #addressesToCandidates() helper to
  eliminate duplicated address-to-CommissionableDevice synthesis in
  commission() and establishPase()
- CommissioningConnectionPool: document permanentlyExcluded param as
  reserved for future dynamic-discovery polling
- CommissioningConnectionTest: increase timeout margins (5ms→50ms,
  100ms→1000ms) for resilience on slow CI runners
- Import ordering fixes (formatter)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: defer onDiscovered for new ClientNodes until construction.ready; add unit tests

The discovered.on callback in ContinuousDiscovery could fire before the
CommissioningClient.initialize transaction committed, leaving
node.state.commissioning with the default empty state.  Split the
descriptor callback into two paths: existing nodes (already constructed)
emit immediately, newly created nodes defer onDiscovered via
construction.ready so all state is readable by listeners.

Add unit tests:
- CommissioningConnectionPoolTest: candidates, in-flight exclusion,
  markInvalidCredentials permanence, address replacement, attempt key
  uniqueness, and batch address merging (6 tests).
- RemoteDescriptorTest: toLongForm/fromLongForm roundtrip for addresses,
  deviceIdentifier, discriminator/CM, VP, empty input, and a regression
  test verifying a minimal CommissionableDevice does not roundtrip to {}
  (6 tests).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* formatting

* fix: restore 30s default discovery timeout in CommissioningDiscovery

The old discoverAndEstablishPase enforced a 30s timeout by default.
CommissioningDiscovery had no default, causing discovery to run
indefinitely when no timeout was passed (e.g. from LegacyWebSocket
PairWithCode path).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* refactor: address code review findings on commissioning PR

- Extract ParallelPaseDiscovery<W> base class; CommissioningDiscovery and
  PaseDiscovery now extend it, eliminating the duplicated #paseWon / #pending /
  #abort / #winnerPromise race-gate pattern
- Fix discoveryCapabilities silently dropped: CommissioningDiscovery constructor
  now converts discoveryCapabilities to a scannerFilter, matching the legacy
  CommissioningController / PaseCommissioner behaviour for BLE callers
- Extract shared discoverCommissionableDevices / cancelDiscoverCommissionableDevices
  helpers in CommissioningController; PaseCommissioner delegates to them, removing
  the 30-line duplication
- Remove dead permanentlyExcluded parameter from CommissioningConnectionPool.availableCandidates
- Extract LOSER_CLEANUP_BUDGET_MS constant in CommissioningConnection (was magic 5000)
- Clean up now-unused imports in PaseCommissioner

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* formatting

* fix(ble): two bugs in NobleBleChannel connect/disconnect path

**Missing return after successful channel creation**
After resolveOnce() succeeds in connectHandler the service loop continued
to completion, fell through to the rejectOnce() fallback intended for the
"no Matter characteristics found" case, and logged:
  "Already resolved or rejected, ignore error: Peripheral … does not have
   the required Matter characteristics"
Add return; in the success path so the fallback is only reached when the
loop genuinely finds no suitable service.

**Swapped error labels + unsubscribe race on BTP session close**
The error callbacks for unsubscribeAsync() and disconnectAsync() had their
log labels swapped.  Additionally, when the remote side closes the BTP
session first Noble fires the disconnect event and its _withDisconnectHandler
rejects any pending BLE operation (including unsubscribeAsync) with
"Disconnected unknown".  The previous error handler just logged and stopped,
never calling disconnectAsync().
Fix: use .catch() so an unsubscribe failure is logged but the code still
proceeds to disconnectAsync() if the peripheral is still connected.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: empty BLE deviceIdentifier causing re-commissioning of existing node

On macOS CoreBluetooth hides real BLE peripheral addresses, so the BLE
scanner emits deviceIdentifier: "" for every discovered device.

RemoteDescriptor.is() treated "" as a valid identifier ("" !== undefined),
so factory.find() could match it against a previously commissioned node
that was also discovered via BLE (also stored deviceIdentifier: "").
CommissioningDiscovery.onDiscovered() would then receive the old
commissioned node and CommissioningClient.commission() would throw
"is already commissioned".

Fixes:
- RemoteDescriptor.is(): treat empty-string deviceIdentifier the same as
  absent — an empty string means "identity unknown" and must not be used
  to match unrelated nodes.
- Discovery.ts: second safety net — if factory.find() returns a node that
  is already commissioned (node.lifecycle.isCommissioned), treat it as no
  match and let the flow create a fresh node.
- CommissioningClient.ts: filter BLE addresses out of the "Operational
  address changed" log line; ble:// is meaningless on macOS and clutters
  logs with empty-string addresses.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix(polyfill): SuppressedError installed as arrow function, breaking new E()

TypeScript's __callDispose polyfill (generated for await using) calls
new E(...) where E = globalThis.SuppressedError.  The suppressed-error
polyfill was installing an arrow function (factory) as the global, but
arrow functions cannot be used as constructors.  This caused:
  "E is not a constructor"
whenever two errors occurred simultaneously during an await using scope
(e.g. commissioning reconnect failure + failsafe reset failure), crashing
the cleanup path instead of wrapping both errors in a SuppressedError.

Fix: install the class directly.  The factory pattern added no value —
TypeScript's generated code always uses new, and instanceof checks work
correctly against the class itself.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix

commit.message:
fix: review endpoint now uses MCP/Work IQ for research-based responses

Previously, POST /api/tasks/:id/review used createSession({}) without
MCP — the AI could only accept user assumptions, never verify them.
Now uses Work IQ MCP so the agent can search emails/Teams when the
user asks to verify, analyze, or research something. Prompt instructs
AI to distinguish simple answers (resolve directly) from research
requests (search first, then resolve with evidence). Timeout increased
from 30s to 180s.

commit.message:
fix: prevent concurrent operations from overwriting frozen task cards

renderTasks() now preserves DOM elements for frozen tasks instead of
replacing them. When an agent is working on a task (frozen state),
fetchTasks() from another operation (e.g. merge) no longer overwrites
the card with stale server data. The detail panel is also protected
when the selected task is frozen. This prevents race conditions where
completing one operation resets another in-progress operation's UI.

Use recency-weighted scoring for TicTacToe team selection

Replace raw player-count ranking with score = players × recency_factor,
where recency_factor decays 5% per year since the team's last active
season (floored at 10%). This ensures currently active teams like
Monaco, Paris, Dubai rank above long-inactive ones like Olimpija,
Cibona, Prokom.

season display name (team_name_that_season) instead of stale team.name.

Expand Imperator/CK3 mod detection via configurables instead of hardcoded logic (#2990)

Mod detection was entirely hardcoded (`StartsWith("The Fallen Eagle")`,
etc.) and file-tweaker blocks were gated on hardcoded boolean properties
per mod. This makes adding new mod support require C# changes. Replaces
both with configurable-driven detection.

## New configurable format

`configurables/ck3_mods.txt` and `configurables/imperator_mods.txt`
define detection rules:

```
tfe = {
    name_regex = { "^The Fallen Eagle" }
    id = { "ugc_2243307127.mod" }
}
```

A flag is activated if any loaded mod's name matches any regex **or**
its path ends with any listed ID. Both fields are optional.

## Key changes

- **`CommonUtils/ModDefinition.cs`** — New `ModDefinition` and
`ModDefinitionsReader` classes; parses the configurable format, regex
compiled with 1s timeout (ReDoS protection)
- **`configurables/ck3_mods.txt`** — Definitions for `tfe`, `wtwsms`,
`roa`, `aep`
- **`configurables/imperator_mods.txt`** — Definitions for `invictus`,
`invictus_1_7`, `terra_indomita`
- **`Configuration.cs`** — `DetectSpecificCK3Mods` now loads
`ck3_mods.txt`; `FallenEagleEnabled` etc. become computed properties
from a `HashSet<string> activeCK3ModFlags`; `GetCK3ModFlags()` builds
dynamically from loaded definitions so any new flag in the file
automatically propagates to Liquid templates.
`DetectSpecificImperatorMods` moved here from `Imperator/World.cs`;
`InvictusDetected`, `Invictus1_7Detected`, `TerraIndomitaDetected` are
now computed properties on `Configuration`; `AddImperatorModFlag()`
added for fallback detection from save data; `GetLiquidVariables()` now
includes Imperator mod flags with an `ir_` prefix (e.g. `ir_invictus`,
`ir_terra_indomita`)
- **`Imperator/World.cs`** — Calls
`config.DetectSpecificImperatorMods(UsableMods)` after mod loading;
save-data fallback detection (global flag `is_playing_invictus`, country
`unification_points` variable) handled via
`config.AddImperatorModFlag()`
- **`CK3/World.cs`** — Uses `config.InvictusDetected`,
`config.Invictus1_7Detected`, `config.TerraIndomitaDetected` instead of
reading from `irWorld`
- **`FileTweaker.cs`** — Hardcoded `if (config.FallenEagleEnabled)`
blocks replaced with a loop over `config.GetActiveCK3ModFlags()`,
loading `removable_file_blocks_{flag}.txt` /
`replaceable_file_blocks_{flag}.txt` when they exist — new mods need
only a configurable entry and the block files, no C# changes

## Backward compatibility

Existing boolean properties and flag keys (`tfe`, `vanilla`, etc.) are
preserved. `ThrowUserErrorExceptionForUnsupportedModCombinations` is
unchanged.

commit.message:
fix: suppress numpy uint8 overflow warnings in BFS flood-fill

The BFS compared pixel channels with int(pr)-nr where nr was still
np.uint8. NumPy treats Python int as a 'weak' type and promotes the
result to uint8, causing underflow wrap-around and RuntimeWarnings.

Fix: extract all channel values as Python int() upfront so all
arithmetic stays in Python-int space (no overflow possible).

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Achieve enterprise accessibility and navigation parity for MVP commercialization (#586)

* Initial plan

* Implement WCAG 2.1 AA accessibility and navigation parity improvements

Co-authored-by: ludovit-scholtz <256357527+ludovit-scholtz@users.noreply.github.com>

* Fix low-signal E2E test assertions across 5 spec files

Replace always-true assertions (expect(true).toBe(true), expect(x || true).toBe(true),
expect(count).toBeGreaterThanOrEqual(0)) with meaningful assertions:

- portfolio-intelligence.spec.ts:
  * Import suppressBrowserErrors; replace inline page.on() in beforeEach
  * 'no add button' fallback → assert heading still visible
  * 'dialog not visible' fallback → assert dialog.not.toBeVisible()
  * keyboard nav → body.click() + Tab + document.activeElement check
  * visible || true → anyInsight.isVisible() (redundant OR removed)

- accessibility-first-launch.spec.ts:
  * Import suppressBrowserErrors; add beforeEach to each describe block
  * Remove ~18 inline page.on() handlers
  * alertCount >= 0 → renderedHTML.toContain('role="alert"') (real template check)
  * buttonCount >= 0 → buttonCount >= 1 (any rendered auth page has ≥1 button)

- accessibility-auth-launch.spec.ts:
  * Replace all 3 beforeEach inline suppressors with suppressBrowserErrors(page)
  * 'no form visible' → check email input or sign-in button visible
  * 'form behind auth' fallback → re-assert heading visible
  * keyboard fallback → body.click() + Tab + document.activeElement check

- accessibility-conversion-hardening.spec.ts:
  * aria-live count >= 0 → ariaLiveAttached || h1Present (real structural check)

- compliance-setup-workspace.spec.ts:
  * hasBlocker || true → summaryHeading re-assert + meaningful OR

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* refactor(e2e): replace inline error suppressors with suppressBrowserErrors helper

Replace inline page.on('console',...) / page.on('pageerror',...) error-suppression
handlers with the canonical suppressBrowserErrors(page) helper in 14 E2E spec files.

- Add import { suppressBrowserErrors } from './helpers/auth' to each file
- Remove local suppressPageErrors function from issuance-policy-guardrails.spec.ts
- Preserve functional page.on handlers that collect errors for assertions
  (errors.push, jsErrors.push, analyticsEvents capture)

Files changed: lifecycle-cockpit, portfolio-launchpad, token-portfolio-clarity,
guided-portfolio-onboarding, subscription-billing, business-command-center,
trustworthy-operations-ux, compliance-dashboard, competitive-telemetry-integration,
issuance-policy-guardrails, auth-error-scenarios, vision-insights-workspace,

Fix syntax errors in 4 files where the edit tool merged the import line

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix(a11y): v-show error banner for WCAG 4.1.3 aria-live region; fix 3 CI failures

Co-authored-by: ludovit-scholtz <256357527+ludovit-scholtz@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix(e2e): fix issuance-policy-guardrails alert banner test for v-show pattern

The test 'error banner element has correct ARIA role when present' was checking
alertBanners.count() > 0 to gate a toBeVisible() assertion. With v-show (WCAG 4.1.3
compliant pattern keeping aria-live regions always in DOM), count() returns 1 even
when the banner is hidden by CSS, causing toBeVisible() to fail on clean page load.

Fix: use toBeAttached() to verify the WCAG requirement (region always in DOM), then
use isVisible() as the gate before checking text content. Also adds positive assertions
for role="alert" and aria-live="assertive" attributes that are always present.
This is a stronger test that directly validates the WCAG 4.1.3 implementation.

Co-authored-by: ludovit-scholtz <256357527+ludovit-scholtz@users.noreply.github.com>

- Remove dead code (if block was unreachable after not.toBeVisible assertion)
